label: Phase 1: Detox
description: Flat baseline — clean eating habits, no differentiation by day yet. Alcohol desserts and biscuits cut. Training load is moderate; the goal is consistency and gut health.

label: Phase 2: Building
description: First real differentiation. Rest days stay low, training days step up. Longer long runs (28k peak) mean Sunday fuelling increases noticeably. Body learns to expect more on hard days.

label: Phase 3: Peak load
description: Highest sustained training load. 32k peak on 24 May — biggest calorie day of the entire plan. Bike + hike + long run weeks stack up. Fuel aggressively especially Friday-Sunday.

label: Phase 4: Taper + Race
description: Volume drops sharply but calories stay moderate-high — carb loading kicks in Wed-Sat. Race day is 3800 kcal with gels during. Protect sleep, cut alcohol, eat the pasta.

label: Phase 5: Recovery
description: Post-marathon rebuild. Easy running, short hikes. Calories settle back to ~2000 avg. No pressure on pace or volume — just move eat well and let the body absorb what you did.
